Name: Match Recording → Scouting Pipeline
Description: Systematically record tournament matches with tools like SwingVision, extract patterns, and generate a coaching brief before the next tournament. The highlight reel is a byproduct that feeds the scouting workflow. This is the highest-leverage thing after the app because it directly attacks the visibility problem — athletes currently have no structured video evidence when applying to colleges.
Effort: Operational more than technical — deploy existing tools, build the workflow.
Potential: Proves the core thesis. One athlete with a real profile is the case study.
Why it matters: US college coaches recruit on video. Mexican juniors don't have it in any organized form.

Name: Highlight Distribution for Visibility
Description: Automate clip generation and distribution to multiple platforms (YouTube, social, college recruiting platforms) from match recordings. WSC Sports does this for leagues — the academy can replicate the workflow manually/semi-automatically using AI video tools.
Effort: Medium — depends on tooling available and match recording infrastructure being in place first.
Potential: If this works at the academy level, it's a service other Mexican clubs would pay for.
Why it matters: In Mexico there is no broadcasting or highlight DB for junior tennis. Athletes are invisible to scouts unless they create their own content.

Name: Knowledge Graph — capa de señal humana del expediente
Description: Grafo de conocimiento (inspirado en GBrain de Garry Tan: markdown-first, entidades y edges tipados extraídos sin LLM, retrieval híbrido semántico + grafo) construido sobre el contenido narrativo del expediente del atleta — PTFs, notas de carácter del coach, reportes de psico/nutri/fisio. No reemplaza el schema relacional en Supabase, que sigue siendo la fuente de verdad para el expediente verificable; es una capa de retrieval/reasoning encima para permitir preguntas en lenguaje natural con evidencia cruzada entre fuentes.
Effort: Alto y prematuro por ahora. Depende de que el MVP relacional esté estable y de tener volumen real de PTFs/reportes narrativos acumulados antes de que el retrieval de grafo aporte valor.
Potential: Habilita un asistente de AI que responda preguntas sobre un atleta cruzando evidencia narrativa + estructurada — diferenciador fuerte de cara a recruiters y universidades. Origen: conversación con founder de http://ai-coach.fr sobre GBrain, 2026-07-08.
Why it matters: Ataca directamente la capa diferenciadora del producto (la señal humana que ningún ranking como UTR/AMTP entrega). Los datos numéricos — ranking, SwingVision — no se benefician de un grafo porque ya son estructurados; el valor del grafo está específicamente en el texto libre y sus relaciones.

Name: AI-Driven Coaching Personalization
Description: Use match analysis data to generate personalized practice plans before the next tournament. If patterns from a match are captured (e.g., weak second serve under pressure, poor return positioning), training sessions can be adapted around those specific gaps.
Effort: Depends on match recording pipeline. Once data exists, prompting an AI to generate a training plan is low-effort.
Potential: Differentiator for the academy vs. traditional coaching.
Why it matters: Connects data to on-court behavior change — the core value prop of a high-performance academy.

Name: Structured Athlete Scouting Profile
Description: A profile per athlete combining match video, performance trends, and coach notes — formatted for US college recruiters. Currently, athletes send a highlight reel assembled two months before the application deadline. This turns it into an ongoing, data-backed artifact.
Effort: Builds on match recording pipeline. Mostly curation and format design.
Potential: Could become a service for other academies in Mexico.
Why it matters: Fills the gap between "talented kid in Mexico" and "recruitable player with evidence."

Name: Scouting-as-a-Service for Mexican Athletes
Description: Expand the academy's pipeline to serve other Mexican high schools and clubs with interest in sending athletes to the US or Europe. Replaces fragmented, human-driven scouting networks with a systematic video + profile + outreach workflow.
Effort: High — requires the academy pipeline to be proven first.
Potential: This is where it becomes a business beyond the academy.
Why it matters: The academy has the proof of concept. Other clubs have the athletes but not the infrastructure.
